meaning: adj.粗鲁的,愚钝的,非常的,粗糙的
examples: However, the magazines also depicted music halls—which competed for patronage with all theaters—as places where crass entertainment corrupted spectators' taste and morals.
That looks extreme, but abuse can be crass.
The fans don't like crass commercialism, Angelos believes, so the ballpark isn't for sale.
They can be crass, overstate their influence, screw up badly because they are inexperienced.
